Class PIITokenizer

java.lang.Object
com.semanticprivacyguard.tokenizer.PIITokenizer
public final class PIITokenizer extends Object
Replaces detected PII spans in text with structured replacement tokens.

Token format

Tokens take the form [TYPE_N] where TYPE is the PIIType.getLabel() and N is a 1-based counter scoped to each type within a single redaction call. Examples:

  • [EMAIL_1], [EMAIL_2]
  • [PERSON_NAME_1]
  • [SSN_1]

The token format is deliberately chosen so that an LLM receiving the redacted text can still understand the sentence structure, infer the type of missing information, and work with the anonymised context — without ever seeing the original values.

De-tokenisation

The redact(String, List) method returns a Map<String,String> that maps each replacement token back to the original value, enabling authorised downstream systems to reconstruct the original text if needed.

Redaction modes

Three built-in modes are supported, configurable at construction time:

  • PIITokenizer.RedactionMode.TOKEN — structured [TYPE_N] tokens (default; ideal for LLM pipelines).
  • PIITokenizer.RedactionMode.MASK — replaces the value with a fixed-width sequence of █ characters.
  • PIITokenizer.RedactionMode.BLANK — replaces the value with a single [REDACTED] placeholder.
Since:
1.0.0
Author:
Hemant Naik

    PIITokenizer.RedactionMode
    Determines how detected PII is represented in the output.

    Holds the redacted text string and the token → original reverse map.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PIITokenizer()
    Creates a tokenizer using PIITokenizer.RedactionMode.TOKEN (default).

    Creates a tokenizer using the specified redaction mode.

  • Constructor Details

    • PIITokenizer

      public PIITokenizer()
      Creates a tokenizer using PIITokenizer.RedactionMode.TOKEN (default).

    • PIITokenizer

      public PIITokenizer(PIITokenizer.RedactionMode mode)
      Creates a tokenizer using the specified redaction mode.
      Parameters:
      mode - the redaction mode (never null)

  • Method Details

    • redact

      public PIITokenizer.RedactionOutput redact(String text, List<PIIMatch> matches)
      Applies all matches to text, returning the redacted string and a reverse-lookup map.

      The caller is responsible for ensuring that matches are non-overlapping and sorted by start index (as guaranteed by CompositeDetector).

      Parameters:
      text - the original text (never null)
      matches - non-overlapping, sorted list of PII matches
      Returns:
      a PIITokenizer.RedactionOutput containing the redacted text and the token-to-original reverse map
